title: sedum radiatum subsp. ciliosum
intro: Sedum radiatum subsp. ciliosum, also known as the Spiny Stonecrop, is a succulent plant species in the Crassulaceae family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The Spiny Stonecrop has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, improving wound healing, and even showing potential in reducing the growth of certain cancer cells. Sedum radiatum subsp. ciliosum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
